name: Don Lamond
abstract: Donald Douglas Lamond Jr. (August 18, 1920 – December 23, 2003) was an American jazz drummer.

works.t: Strike Up the Band: The Man I Love

concerts.label: Woody Herman and His Orchestra and The Nat King Cole Trio
Jazz: The Personal Dimension: Teddy Wilson Trio
Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others
Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others
Woody Herman and His Thundering Herd: 40th Anniversary Concert
Kool Jazz Festival: An Evening of the Music of Harold Arlen

about_quotes.speaker: Carmen Leggio
about_quotes.evidence: In the group was Zoot Sims, myself, Joe Newman, Billy Taylor, Kai Winding was the leader, Don Lamond, Clyde Lombardi and that was it.
about_quotes.doc_title: Carmen Leggio interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Duffy Jackson
about_quotes.evidence: the gentleman that took Davy Tough’s place in the Woody Herman band with my father, his name is Don Lamond... he definitely taught me a lot about just the technique of the drumset.
about_quotes.doc_title: Duffy Jackson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, October 17, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Dan D'Imperio
about_quotes.evidence: And Jake, the three drummers, with me, Jake Hanna and Don Lamond. I’ve got a picture of the three of us together. We shared the drum chairs on that.

about_quotes.speaker: Marian McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: I had great sidemen — Ed Safranski from Kenton’s band and Don Lamond from Woody’s band, Woody Herman’s band... that was my first trio group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marian McPartland interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, April 26, 1997

about_quotes.speaker: Pete Candoli
about_quotes.evidence: Don Lamond was on the band at that time instead of Davy Tough... And I came in on my high C
about_quotes.doc_title: Pete Candoli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 15, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Rusty Dedrick
about_quotes.evidence: And the rhythm section was known at that time as the NBC rhythm section because they were all on staff. That was Mondello, Dick Hyman, Don Lamond and Eddie Safranski. And we just went in and played.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rusty Dedrick interviewed by Monk Rowe, Ellenville, New York, June 23, 1998
